dial.summary: Net score holds at +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 ~$102.7T on an intact 4-week uptrend of ~$1.0T, Fed balance sheet $6.74T flat with no aggressive QT, BTC funding rates near zero at +0.01%, stablecoin dominance 13.58% on a confirmed 30-day downtrend from ~14.3–14.5% in mid-June; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above 50 but falling 4-week with next release August 3, 2s10s +0.40% positive but flat over 30 days; 2 headwinds: DXY 100.92 still above the 100 neutral-band ceiling despite easing on soft CPI, real yields 2.33% elevated and rising 4-week); June 2026 CPI came in materially below consensus—headline 3.5% YoY (vs 3.8–3.9% expected) and core 2.6% YoY (vs 2.9% expected)—validating the disinflationary pipeline flagged by ISM's 9.1pp price collapse in June and reinforcing the BTC Accumulation thesis, with DXY needing to sustain a close below 100 and real yields to compress below 2% before the two remaining headwinds flip.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: MacroMicro 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at ~$102.65T (~$102.7T) through May 2026—the latest available data point given the 6–8 week reporting lag; 4-week trend up: rose ~$1.0T from ~$101.7T (~30 days ago) to ~$102.7T, a +~1.0% gain well above the flat threshold; June 2026 data expected to become available in coming weeks; status supportive (4-week trend clearly up, YoY growth positive).

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI confirmed at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive month of expansion but 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (+1.1pp, still contracting), prices 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022, consistent with June CPI's significant undershoot vs consensus); no July 2026 data yet—next ISM Manufacturing PMI for July releases August 3, 2026; 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires both above 50 AND rising).

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY at ~100.92 on July 15, 2026, down ~0.38% on the day (intraday low 100.60) after June CPI printed materially below consensus (headline 3.5% YoY vs 3.8–3.9% expected, core 2.6% vs 2.9%); hawkish Warsh Senate testimony partially reversed the initial CPI-driven selloff; 4-week trend up—DXY was near ~100.0 on June 11–12 (pre-FOMC) and is now ~100.92, a +~0.92% gain over 4 weeks, above the 0.5% flat threshold; status headwind (ICE DXY at 100.92 remains above the 100 neutral-band ceiling; a sustained close below 100 is required to flip to neutral).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.33% as of July 14, 2026 (-0.01pp from July 13); 4-week trend up—yield has risen ~0.19pp over the past month (from ~2.14% on June 12 to 2.33%); soft June CPI (headline 3.5% YoY, core 2.6%) released July 14 caused nominal 10Y to fall to 4.583% and 2Y to 4.185%—breakeven inflation expectations rebounded alongside nominal yields, leaving real yields roughly flat on the day; status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis; a sustained decline below 2% is required to flip to neutral).

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at 4.583% and 2-year at 4.185% as of July 14, 2026—spread +0.398% (~+0.40%); 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.38–0.40% in mid-June (pre-FOMC range), now +0.40%, a change of ~0–2bps well within the flat threshold (<5bps); the soft CPI print on July 14 steepened the curve intraday as the 2-year (more rate-sensitive) fell more sharply (-7bps) than the 10-year (-2bps), widening the spread from +0.35% (July 10) to +0.40%; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the -50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold, but not yet sustaining a steepening trend—supportive requires positive AND steepening).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: H.4.1 balance sheet for week ended July 9, 2026 (released July 10): total assets $6,736B ($6.74T); next H.4.1 release July 17 at 4:30 PM ET for week ended July 16, 2026; 4-week comparison: week ended ~June 10 estimated ~$6.71T; 4-week change ~+$30B / +0.45%, within the flat threshold; status supportive (balance sheet flat with no acceleration of QT, no aggressive reserve drain).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC perpetual funding rate near +0.01% per 8-hour interval (Binance/Bybit/OKX aggregate; latest CoinGlass/Convex data through late June–early July 2026); near-zero funding during BTC's rally on soft CPI day (July 14) confirms spot-driven dynamics rather than overleveraged longs, consistent with a healthy accumulation phase; 30-day trend flat (funding has held near zero throughout June and into July); status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold).

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap ~$307B against total crypto market cap ~$2.26T as of mid-July 2026 (CoinGecko: ~13.58%); down from ~13.73% on July 14 and from ~14.3–14.5% in mid-June, the 30-day downtrend remains clearly intact indicating net capital rotation into the broader crypto ecosystem; BTC.D firm at 56.3% while stablecoin dominance trends lower 30-day is consistent with BTC accumulation rather than broad altcoin rotation; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; headwind condition requires rising while TOTAL also rising, which does not apply on a 30-day basis).

upcoming.title: Retail Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau Advance Monthly Retail Trade for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—key test of consumer spending health ahead of the July 28–29 FOMC; real yields at 2.33% and the 10-year at ~4.58% continue to weigh on big-ticket discretionary spending; a solid print reinforces the soft-landing narrative; Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Survey (July 2026) also at 8:30 AM ET—second early-Q3 regional factory read; H.4.1 Fed balance sheet (week ended July 9) may release at 4:30 PM ET providing the next balance sheet update.

upcoming.title: Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 12, 2026)
upcoming.note: Weekly UI claims at 8:30 AM ET—released same morning as retail sales; two consecutive weeks above 240K would mark the first concrete signal of broad labor deterioration and reinforce rate-cut expectations ahead of the July 28–29 FOMC; the paired labor-and-spending release on July 16 provides the clearest simultaneous read on early-Q3 consumer and labor health before the FOMC blackout period (beginning July 18) limits further Fed commentary.

upcoming.title: Housing Starts & Building Permits / Industrial Production / UMich Sentiment (June–July 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau new residential construction for June at 8:30 AM ET—housing demand signal in the still-elevated real-yield environment (2.33%); Federal Reserve Industrial Production & Capacity Utilization (June) at 9:15 AM ET cross-checks the ISM Manufacturing expansion (53.3%, sixth consecutive month above 50); University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment preliminary (July) at 10:00 AM ET—watch the 1-year and 5-year inflation expectations components, which carry outsized weight for the Warsh Fed's reaction function; these are the last significant releases before the FOMC blackout begins July 18; H.4.1 Fed balance sheet expected at 4:30 PM ET.

upcoming.title: NAHB Housing Market Index (July 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Home Builders / Wells Fargo Housing Market Index for July 2026 at 10:00 AM ET—builder sentiment leading indicator; FOMC blackout runs July 18–30, so no Fed commentary accompanies this week's releases; the major events capping the window are the July 23 ECB rate decision, the July 28–29 FOMC meeting (decision July 29 at 2:00 PM ET, no dot plot), and the July 30 trio of Q2 GDP advance estimate, PCE, and BOE rate decision.

upcoming.title: Existing Home Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Realtors (NAR) Existing Home Sales for June 2026—measures closings on previously owned homes and reflects mortgage lock-in dynamics in a still-elevated rate environment (10Y ~4.58%, real yields 2.33%); released during the FOMC blackout (July 18–30) so no Fed commentary amplifies the print; one trading day before the ECB rate decision on July 23.
